title: Why do the Angels always attack in the daytime
display_title: Why do the Angels always attack in the daytime
wikitext:
[]: Why do the Angels always attack in the daytime?             Quote Post by Margerald » Tue Sep 11, 2012 4:49 pm Hello! I have just finished the series Neon Genesis Evangelion for the first time, and I have to say, it was an incredible experience. However, there is one thing that makes the entire thing look kind of silly... Why do the Angels only attack in the day? NERV is always prepared to dispatch the Evangelion units to combat the Angels in the day time, and that's when the Angels always come. Why don't the Angels try a more logical approach by catching the humans off guard late at night? The pilots would likely be at their homes sleeping and NERV headquarters unprepared for such an event. Post your thoughts and opinions as well as actual facts regarding the topic. I look forward to reading them.

[]:             Quote Post by Dream » Tue Sep 11, 2012 4:57 pm I don't remember it's name, but the angel from episode 11 attacked as the day went on, the episode ended at night. Ramiel attacked for an entire day. That's from the top of my head. Now, as for why they all seem to start their attacks during daytime, probably just a funny coincidence. NERV is prepared to dispatch the Evangelions at any time, 24/7. They are humanity's guardians. NERV likely has an entire personnel group and a bazillion plans for a "angel attacking at night" scenario, and outside of Kaworu it's questionable how much self-awareness or agency the angels have, they don't seem much advanced from a little child or a confused teenager.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Tue Sep 11, 2012 5:06 pm Having them all attack during the day does conveniently make it so they don't have to use different bridge crew, since it would seem odd if the command center was staffed by the same people 24 hours a day.

[]:             Quote Post by Dream » Tue Sep 11, 2012 5:13 pm Ornette wrote: Having them all attack during the day does conveniently make it so they don't have to use different bridge crew, since it would seem odd if the command center was staffed by the same people 24 hours a day.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Tue Sep 11, 2012 5:23 pm I think there's also the added bonus of depicting action being easier. There's a couple of fights that drag out into the night (Sachi and Ramiel) but they're exception. I don't think there are any deeper reasons behind it

[]:             Quote Post by Margerald » Tue Sep 11, 2012 6:18 pm I acknowledge some of the fights drag on into the night, but as a poster previously stated none of them actually started at nighttime. It's strange that the angels wouldn't try to catch them off guard at least once by attacking late in the night...

[]:             Quote Post by Dream » Tue Sep 11, 2012 6:29 pm Margerald wrote: I acknowledge some of the fights drag on into the night, but as a poster previously stated none of them actually started at nighttime. It's strange that the angels wouldn't try to catch them off guard at least once by attacking late in the night...

[]:             Quote Post by Margerald » Tue Sep 11, 2012 6:35 pm ^ Hmm, good point.

[]:             Quote Post by svenge » Tue Sep 11, 2012 7:01 pm Some of y'all are overthinking this. There's only 2 major reasons that most Angels came during the day: 1) Economy of characters . Why would anyone want to go through the trouble of creating character designs and cast VAs for the third-shift bridge crew if they didn't have to? This applies to live-action films as well. In Apollo 13, they basically depicted one shift of the Mission Control staff throughout the film, with a small scene showing a hand-off to a second shift. In reality, there were 4 separate daily shifts that worked that mission. However, it would be asking a lot of the audience to keep track of all those "redundant" (in terms of functionality) characters, so they focused on just one group. 2) Efficiency of production . While Sachiel was defeated during the night, that battle was literally one-dimensional (as Eva-01 was inserted into the battle via elevator right in front of it) so depicting the locations and actions of the battle in the darkness wasn't too difficult. The same can be said for Operation Yashima, which was essentially a static sniper action against Ramiel. Most of the other battles had movement in at least two dimensions, which means that high visibility (read: daylight) is required to convey the action to the viewers effectively. When you get down to it, the time of day that an Angel attacks has almost nothing to do with internal in-universe logic, but production logistics instead. It's like what I said regarding the nit-picking of the tactics used in the Armisael battle resulting in Rei II's death : Sometimes the meta answer is the only right answer, however unsatisfying it may be.

[]:             Quote Post by Margerald » Tue Sep 11, 2012 7:19 pm You've made some very good points in that post. In the show, it would only seem logical for the Angels to strike during the night, but creating a new set of characters at headquarters would prove troublesome for the writers and confusing for the viewers. Plus, as a previous poster has stated, Angels may not even have the same thought process as a human being. Thank you for taking the time to write up that post.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Tue Sep 11, 2012 7:33 pm Didn't Bardiel only attack during sunset? Anyway, the meta answer mentioned above is sufficient. Also, I like to think Angels have a sleep cycle as well. They are living beings, after all. NME suggests that Angels might come from the moon. The Angels' time of arrival in Tokyo III may have more to do with the position of the moon in relation to the point in Earth the Angels' are interested in invading and the time it takes for the Angels to travel from the moon to the Earth. If we knew where the angels came from in NGE, we could perhaps put an in-world answer to this question.

[]:             Quote Post by Dream » Tue Sep 11, 2012 7:36 pm FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: NME tells us that Angels come from the moon. They Angels' time of arrival in Tokyo III may have more to do with the position of the moon in relation to the point in Earth the Angels' are interested in invading. If we knew where the angels came from in NGE, we could perhaps put an in-world answer to this question.

[]:             Quote Post by Der Kommissar » Tue Sep 11, 2012 7:44 pm There's no reason to assume the Angels even know the difference between day and night, let alone the significance of night to the Lilin. Further, even if they had, nighttime affords no advantage against a paramilitary organization with a black budget approved by the secret rulers of the world that outstrips some countries.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Tue Sep 11, 2012 10:07 pm Uh...guys? Maybe the Angels sleep during the night. Like humans. Because they technically are humans. And we know Angels do 'sleep' to some degree, since Sachiel.

[]:             Quote Post by Der Kommissar » Wed Sep 12, 2012 3:50 am Not all of them, then. Ramiel was willing to pull an all-nighter. 'Wait to see if it falls asleep' would've been an amusing plan, though.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Wed Sep 12, 2012 3:18 pm Lilin humans can pull all-nighters too, dontcha know. Weren't everyone at NERV doing exactly that to make sure Remiel doesn't fuck shit up? It doesn't even matter if you respond with "Angels have infinite energy" since sleep also plays important psychological functions, and if Angels have similar souls to Lilin~...

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Sep 12, 2012 3:24 pm Der Kommissar wrote: There's no reason to assume the Angels even know the difference between day and night

[]:             Quote Post by Der Kommissar » Wed Sep 12, 2012 4:42 pm AuraTwilight wrote: Lilin humans can pull all-nighters too, dontcha know. Weren't everyone at NERV doing exactly that to make sure Remiel doesn't fuck shit up? It doesn't even matter if you respond with "Angels have infinite energy" since sleep also plays important psychological functions, and if Angels have similar souls to Lilin~...

[]:             Quote Post by JoeD80 » Thu Sep 13, 2012 12:28 pm After watching episode 5 last time, I started thinking that the Angels maybe trigger on something NERV is doing at the time. It's the second the activation test with Unit 00 starts that Angel #5 appears and Fuyutsuki gets the call. I think they attack in the day, because NERV is active in the day. With minimal activity at night, the Angel is just puttering around not knowing what to do.

[]:             Quote Post by Pandymint » Thu Sep 13, 2012 2:08 pm I suppose that could be possible, meta-wise. Due to inactivity, the Angel may have no sense of direction to NERV HQ/Lilith. Then once there is a spike of energy, it starts to head that way. But that doesn't necessarily explain how it can pinpoint exactly where Lilith is...
